summary: We recommend reverting the SessionStart hook because the original pull 4 design blocked the Claude Code prompt for tens of seconds waiting for apt and dockerd service health checks which is unacceptable user experience. The tradeoff against patching the hook to be non-blocking in place is that the revert is faster to ship and the operator wants the simplest possible main branch behavior. Subsequent ticks codified the correct non-blocking pattern on the outcomes-mcp repo for orchestrator use cases.

claims.statement: outcomes pull 5 reverted the SessionStart hook commits from pull 4 because the merged diff is the inverse of pull 4 and outcomes main no longer contains the apt or dockerd wait in any session-start path.
claims.caveats: The non-blocking pattern was later codified on outcomes-mcp under A5 with a sentinel guard and fork plus disown.

claims.statement: session-start latency on outcomes main is at or below 100 milliseconds because no shell commands run on session-start in the reverted state.

citations.title: outcomes settings json without a SessionStart hook
A5 latency runroc measuring the non-blocking pattern at near zero

claims.caveats: Latency measurement is approximate; the runroc shows 4 of 5 runs at 0.00 seconds.

open_questions: Should outcomes main adopt the outcomes-mcp A5 non-blocking pattern or stay hook-free?
methodology_notes: Reviewed both pull 4 and pull 5 diffs on outcomes and compared against the A5 non-blocking pattern on outcomes-mcp.
